_comments.tables: Flags markdown tables whose data rows disagree on column count (the validated junk signal -- e.g. a page number leaked in as an extra column). It deliberately does NOT flag all-empty rows (blank form fields) or single-cell numeric rows (point-total subtotals), which are legitimate.
_comments.boilerplate: No-op until boilerplate_patterns is non-empty. Then it fails when any pattern still matches a note body.
_comments.boilerplate_patterns: Regex strings the boilerplate gate checks for. Mirror the list from pipeline.config.json so the gate catches anything the extractor's normalization missed. Example: ["\\*\\*This ebook belongs to .*?\\*\\*"].
